disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Menstrual period is 6 days late and a home urine pregnancy test turned positive about an hour ago
trigger_question_examples: What made you take the test?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My period's about 6 days late, so I did a test tonight and it came up positive straight away.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Mild early symptoms over the past week — morning nausea and breast tenderness
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any other symptoms this week?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, I've been a bit queasy in the mornings and my breasts have felt sore for about a week.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset racing heart and fast breathing that began the moment the result appeared
trigger_question_examples: Tell me what's happening in your body right now?
When did the racing heart start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The second I saw the line my heart started pounding and I couldn't catch my breath — it's been like this for an hour.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No severe unilateral lower abdominal pain — abdomen feels settled
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any pain low down in your tummy, especially on one side?
Any sharp or one-sided belly pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my tummy doesn't hurt at all — it feels normal.

trigger_question_examples: Have you had any vaginal bleeding or spotting?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pain referred to the tip of the shoulder
trigger_question_examples: Any pain up in the tip of your shoulder?
Have you felt any strange shoulder pain?

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Lives alone and has no emotional support present tonight
trigger_question_examples: Is there someone with you tonight?
Who can support you right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I live on my own and there's nobody here with me tonight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The racing heart and breathlessness ease when talked through calmly, with no chest pain, no radiation, no exertional trigger
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any chest pain, or does it spread to your arm or jaw?
Does the feeling settle when you slow your breathing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's no actual chest pain and it doesn't go anywhere — when I slow my breathing it does ease off a bit.
